description: Use this API to initialize a PSSE dynamic simulation for excitation system response ratio test simulations (i.e., in preparation for activity ERUN) and to specify the Channel Output File into which the output channel values are to be recorded during the simulation. This API routine was first introduced in release 30.2.0. ierr = estr_response_ratio_test(option, pfdefalt, nmachs, busary, idary, pfary, outfile)
python_syntax: ierr = estr_response_ratio_test(option, pfdefalt, nmachs, busary, idary, pfary, outfile)

parameters.name: option
parameters.description: Bus flag indicating those buses to be tested. (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: pfdefalt
parameters.description: Default power factor at which machines are initialized. (input; 0.95 by default).

parameters.name: nmachs
parameters.description: Number of machines to be initialized at other power factors. (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: busary
parameters.description: An array of NMACHS elements specifying a list of bus numbers identifying machines to be initialized at other power factors; ignored if NMACHS = 0. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: idary
parameters.description: An array of NMACHS elements specifying a list of machine identifiers identifying machines to be initialized at other power factors; ignored if NMACHS = 0. (input; ‘1’ by default).

parameters.name: pfary
parameters.description: An array of NMACHS elements specifying a list of power factors; ignored if NMACHS = 0. Machine IDARY(n) at bus BUSARY(n) is initialized to the power factor specified in PFARY(n). (input; PFDEFALT by default).

parameters.name: outfile
parameters.description: Name of the channel output file (blank to bypass recording of the output channel values in a channel output file). (input; blank by default).

option.description: run test on all in-service machines with excitation system models.
bus number for which machines are to be tested.

error_codes.description: prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
